field/object conflation of a different flavor: Q1340623 'Classification of finite simple groups' (CFSG) is a decades-long, ~10,000-page research program classifying ALL finite simple groups up to isomorphism -- Mathlib has NO formalization of CFSG itself (no sporadic-group enumeration, no proof of completeness). `IsSimpleGroup` (Mathlib/GroupTheory/Subgroup/Simple.lean:38, already correctly tagged @[wikidata Q571124] for 'Simple group') only formalizes the DEFINITION of what a simple group is -- the object CFSG classifies, not the classification theorem. concept_layer.jsonl currently lists status='formalized', primary_decl=IsSimpleGroup, confidence=high for this QID, which significantly overclaims (this batch's own collision_groups.json correctly flags status as not_formalized, contradicting concept_layer). Downgrade hard to related; this is the closest legitimate Mathlib entry point (secondary_decls alternatingGroup.isSimpleGroup / CompositionSeries / CommGroup.is_simple_iff_prime_card cover small fragments of CFSG-adjacent results, not the theorem).
